name: libalgorithm-diff-perl
title: libalgorithm-diff-perl
summary: module to find differences between files
description:
module to find differences between files
Algorithm::Diff is a Perl module that allows users to analyze text based on a
Longest Common Subsequence (LCS) algorithm. It can compare two files and find
the differences between them, which can produce the same information as the
common Unix tool 'diff'.
.
There is an XS-optimized implementation of the core loop, which accelerates
some types of diff output (see libalgorithm-diff-xs-perl).
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use libalgorithm-diff-perl because module to find differences between files.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use libalgorithm-diff-perl as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair libalgorithm-diff-perl with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `libalgorithm-diff-perl --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
